physicalDetails.detail: Silver-haired, narrow-shouldered older nobleman in expensive dark wool tailored too finely for ordinary rural life.
His still hands, enchanted warmth, and prepared tea all mark him as a man who has converted wealth into insulation from consequence.

voiceMarkers.detail: Polished old-money courtesy. He answers danger by sounding more refined, never less.
Frames disaster as rumor, loss, and unfortunate scrutiny, preferring administrative language to moral or military language.

beliefs.belief: A crisis can often be survived if it is managed gracefully, framed properly, and kept inside the etiquette of class and ownership.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed as his operating style in chapter 10.

beliefs.belief: Curated access is safer than refusal when outside authority arrives with enough rank to be dangerous.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by the way he turns Jaina over to Jandice instead of denying entry.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Keep the Barov surface composed and survivable while Jaina is absorbed into the scholarly lane beneath his daughter's management.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Protect House Barov's position and avoid becoming the visible face of whatever lies lower than the great hall.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Controlled, detached, and unreadably prepared. Chapter 10 never catches him flustered enough to feel honest.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Receives Jaina without surprise and shifts quickly into the posture of a courteous host under scrutiny.
Lets Jandice take charge of the encounter once the conversation turns from grain politics to scholarship.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Manages suspicion with practiced civility, giving away almost nothing emotionally while still appearing cooperative.
Recognizes when his daughter's intellect is the better defensive instrument than his own political polish.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Jaina Proudmoore
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Uses 'Lord Barov' in a tone that treats the title as leverage rather than respect.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Jandice Barov
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Calls him 'Father' with affectionate impatience and zero submissive softness.

howHeAddressesOthers.pattern: Uses 'Lady Proudmoore' throughout, keeping the encounter inside noble decorum even when she refuses the script.
Uses her given name as both mild warning and familiar concession.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Keeps his hands still on the chair arms even when Jaina hits the real subject, turning calm posture into a denial strategy.
Delegates with a look instead of an argument, signaling long practice in letting Jandice handle the intellectual side of danger.

knowledge.detail: Knows Kirin Tor scrutiny was possible and has a prepared surface response for grain-route suspicion.
Knows Jandice is the house's real scholarly competence center and trusts her to manage a visiting mage better than servants or stewards could.

arc.lie: Control, polish, and inherited authority can keep consequences one layer removed from the self.
arc.truth: Not accepted on page; chapter 10 instead frames him as the polished noble surface over institutional atrocity.
arc.currentPosition: Introduced as the political shell of the Barov arrangement: too prepared, too calm, and too willing to make dangerous questions someone else's department.
